_doc: Task board for TrendFlow Bot (Agent A) — DataProcessor side. Statuses: ⬜ todo, 🔄 in-progress, ✅ done, ⛔ blocked. Plan source: DataProcessor/docs/AGENT_A_WORKPLAN_2026-07-21.md. Agent B board (read-only for A): Models/state/training_tasks.json.
context: 300/300 corpus run complete (DataProcessor/docs/corpus_run_report/). 7 VP components + 2 pipeline steps. NPZ on Network Volume /workspace/corpus_out/<id>/rs/. This likely unblocks Agent B content_features_plus_real_targets + corpus_scale_150_250.

phases.note: DONE. Top-3 cost: cut_detection 25% (CPU only 31%/user 22s of 104s wall -> ~80s NON-compute overhead, biggest lever), scene_classification 22% (CPU-bound resnet50 on CPU due to driver, GPU idle -> move to GPU), core_clip 15% (+ Triton depth/flow, GPU util 8-55% underutilized -> parallelize/binary-tensor). Duration confirmed driver (Spearman 0.8-0.94) except core_clip anomaly (short 7-9s videos slow, 85-99s -> investigate). Memory NOT bottleneck (2.5/16GB) -> CPU is. scene soft-fail 13pz52piBj0 = pre-fix debug artifact, not a code bug.

message_for_agent_b: 300/300 corpus run DONE. NPZ for 7 VP components (core_clip, core_depth_midas, core_optical_flow, cut_detection, scene_classification, video_pacing, uniqueness) on Network Volume: /workspace/corpus_out/<video_id>/rs/<component>/*.npz (2400 files, 8/video). Video list: DataProcessor/docs/corpus_run_report/corpus300.json (300 ids, repo, strata, duration).

These videos are from Ilialebedev/videos* = the SAME pool as Ilialebedev/pre_final_data -> they very likely HAVE real targets. This should unblock your content_features_plus_real_targets + corpus_scale_150_250.

IMPORTANT FORMAT FLAG: my run_corpus.sh wrote NPZ directly into rs/<component>/*.npz WITHOUT a per-run manifest.json (unlike the standard VisualProcessor/main.py orchestrator which writes manifest.json). Your build_training_table.py walks result_store via manifest -> it will NOT find these as-is. Options: (a) I can add a manifest-generation step (regenerate manifests from rs/ dirs), or (b) you add a direct-rs adapter. Tell me which you prefer — quick to add a manifest emitter on my side.
Note: pod is DOWN (killed per owner instruction). NPZ persist on the volume; a new pod must mount the same Network Volume to read them.

phases.note: After phase1. Each optimization: before/after on real videos + NPZ quality diff. Candidates: parallel Triton stages (clip/depth/flow ~190s seq), cut_detection profiling, scene_classification GPU (torch/CUDA compat).
Empirical parallelism stress-test (1/2/3/4 workers), $/1000-videos per GPU option, coordination pattern (adapt Fetcher stable_shard_slot). Cheap pod, short run.

Extend to ~15 addable VP components (excl. object_detections/identity/ocr/text_scoring blocked). Generalize run_corpus.sh (component list as config). Smoke 5-10 videos. Check Docker for micro_emotion/OpenFace.
AudioProcessor(21)+TextProcessor(22) forecast. Blockers: ASR propagation doc.asr, voice_quality config-drift, WavLM cache. Smoke before extrapolating.
Synthesize phases 1-6. All processors, 2-3 machines, 50->500->1000 gate-phases with pass criteria. Disk forecast + HF-offload plan. Multi-machine watchdog.
Per-phase: MB/video added by new components; N videos x MB check vs 120GB volume; HF-offload plan (adapt Fetcher hf_queues.py). Current baseline: 151MB/video on 7 VP comps.
